entities.name: brahman
entities.cite: The one seed does not divide into three parts

entities.name: bhoktṛ
entities.cite: The triad is subject, object, and act: bhoktr, bhoktavya, bhoga

entities.name: bhoktavya
entities.cite: The triad is subject, object, and act: bhoktr, bhoktavya, bhoga

entities.name: bhoga
entities.cite: The triad is subject, object, and act: bhoktr, bhoktavya, bhoga

axioms.name: eka
axioms.cite: The one seed does not divide into three parts; it stands in three modes

axioms.name: sthiti
axioms.cite: its sthiti — its abiding, its standing-as — simply is them

axioms.cite: its sthiti — its abiding, its standing-as — simply is them

axioms.cite: its sthiti — its abiding, its standing-as — simply is them

denials.name: parinama
denials.cite: Bhartrhari's claim is not that Brahman produces these three

denials.name: parinama
denials.cite: Bhartrhari's claim is not that Brahman produces these three

denials.name: parinama
denials.cite: Bhartrhari's claim is not that Brahman produces these three

claims.name: sthiti
claims.cite: in the form of the experiencing

rejected_readings.label: brahman_produces_three
rejected_readings.rendering: Brahman creates and produces the three forms of experiencer, experienced, and experiencing as separate entities.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts parinama (real transformation or production) between brahman and the three, which the commentary explicitly denies: 'Bhartrhari's claim is not that Brahman produces these three but that its sthiti — its abiding, its standing-as — simply is them.' The three are not products but constitute brahman's very standing-as, not the result of transformation.

rejected_readings.label: three_as_properties
rejected_readings.rendering: The experiencer, experienced, and experiencing are merely attributes or properties of brahman, not actual modes of its being.
rejected_readings.why: Treats the three as properties (sort: property) rather than as manifestations (sort: manifestation). This demotes their ontological status. The commentary emphasizes that the three ARE the sthiti of brahman—its actual standing-as—not mere descriptive properties of it. The sort error breaks the axiom.

notes: The contract formalizes the triad doctrine at the heart of 1.4: brahman (undivided absolute) stands in three manifestation-forms (bhoktṛ, bhoktavya, bhoga) without division or transformation. These are powers appearing differentiated yet remaining unified in their single source. The doctrine avoids rigid monism and false dualism alike.
